rationale: This structure partitions Electrical & Electronic Engineering into 11 subspecialties, balancing foundational theory with major application domains. The goal is to align with how ECE departments are structured and how professionals identify themselves. We deliberately kept 'Circuits & Electronics' unified rather than splitting it into analog and digital, as modern VLSI and system-on-chip (SoC) design are inherently mixed-signal. Similarly, 'Power Systems & Electronics' are combined because advances in power electronics are integral to the evolution of modern power grids and renewable energy systems. This count provides sufficient granularity for a specialist without overwhelming a generalist, ensuring a clear home for papers from core theory to applied technology.

subspecialties.name: Circuits & Electronics
subspecialties.tagline: Design of analog, digital, mixed-signal, and RF integrated circuits.
covers: Analysis and design of electronic circuits
VLSI, RFICs, analog-to-digital converters, power management ICs
explicitly_excludes: Digital system architecture → Computer Architecture & Embedded Systems

subspecialties.name: Signal Processing
subspecialties.tagline: Analysis, modification, and synthesis of signals like sound, images, and data.
covers: Digital and statistical signal processing
Image processing, computer vision (EE aspects), audio and speech processing
explicitly_excludes: High-level AI algorithms → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Communication Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Engineering of systems that transmit and receive information.
covers: Wireless communications (5G/6G), optical communications, information theory
Modulation, coding, networking protocols, satellite communications

subspecialties.name: Power Systems & Electronics
subspecialties.tagline: Generation, conversion, and distribution of electric power.
covers: Power generation and transmission, smart grids, renewable energy systems
Power converters, motor drives, electric vehicles

subspecialties.name: Computer Architecture & Embedded Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Design of processors, memory, and specialized computing hardware.
covers: Microprocessor and GPU design, digital systems, hardware description languages (VHDL/Verilog)
Real-time operating systems, IoT devices, hardware security
explicitly_excludes: General algorithms and software → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Photonics & Optics
subspecialties.tagline: Generation, manipulation, and detection of light and its applications.
covers: Lasers, fiber optics, optical sensors, silicon photonics
Displays, imaging systems, quantum optics, nanophotonics
explicitly_excludes: Semiconductor physics of optoelectronics → Semiconductor Devices

subspecialties.name: Semiconductor Devices
subspecialties.tagline: Physics, design, and modeling of solid-state electronic devices.
covers: Transistor physics (MOSFETs, HEMTs), diodes, memory devices
Device modeling, reliability, novel semiconductor materials and structures
explicitly_excludes: Circuit design using devices → Circuits & Electronics

subspecialties.name: Control Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Modeling and controlling dynamical systems using feedback.
covers: Linear and nonlinear control theory, optimal and robust control
State estimation, system identification, applications in aerospace and process control
explicitly_excludes: Power grid control → Power Systems & Electronics

subspecialties.name: Electromagnetics & Antennas
subspecialties.tagline: Theory and application of electromagnetic fields, waves, and devices.
covers: Antenna theory and design, microwave engineering, RF circuits
Computational electromagnetics, wave propagation, electromagnetic compatibility (EMC)
explicitly_excludes: Integrated RF circuit design → Circuits & Electronics

subspecialties.name: Robotics & Automation
subspecialties.tagline: Design and control of robots and automated systems.
covers: Robot kinematics and dynamics, motion planning, sensors and actuators
Industrial automation, autonomous vehicles (hardware/control aspects), human-robot interaction
explicitly_excludes: High-level AI planning → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Bioelectrical Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Application of electrical engineering principles to biology and medicine.
covers: Bioinstrumentation, biosensors, medical imaging (MRI, ultrasound)
Neural engineering, brain-computer interfaces, wearable health devices

uncovered_concerns: Quantum Engineering was considered but not included as a separate category, as its paper volume is currently small and papers often fit within Photonics, Semiconductor Devices, or Control Systems.
Acoustics and MEMS/NEMS were also considered, but are often sub-fields of Signal Processing and Semiconductor Devices/Circuits, respectively, and may be too niche for a level-2 category.
